questions.question: What is the capital of Maharashtra?
questions.explanation: Mumbai is the capital of Maharashtra. It is also the financial capital of India and home to Bollywood.
questions.funFact: Mumbai was originally a group of seven islands that were joined together over many years!

questions.question: Which state is known as the 'Land of Kings'?
questions.explanation: Rajasthan is called the 'Land of Kings' because it was home to many Rajput kings and has beautiful forts and palaces.
questions.funFact: Rajasthan is the largest state in India by area and is bigger than many countries in the world!

questions.question: What is the capital of Tamil Nadu?
questions.explanation: Chennai is the capital of Tamil Nadu. It is a big city on the coast of the Bay of Bengal.
questions.funFact: Chennai was earlier called Madras and is famous for its long Marina Beach, one of the longest urban beaches in the world!

questions.question: Which state is famous for the Taj Mahal?
questions.explanation: The Taj Mahal is in Agra, which is in Uttar Pradesh. It was built by Emperor Shah Jahan.
questions.funFact: The Taj Mahal changes colour at different times of the day — it looks pinkish in the morning, white during the day, and golden at night under the moonlight!

questions.question: What is the capital of Karnataka?
questions.explanation: Bengaluru is the capital of Karnataka. It is known as the IT capital of India because many technology companies are there.
questions.funFact: Bengaluru is called the 'Garden City' because it has so many parks and trees!

questions.question: Which state is known for its beautiful backwaters and coconut trees?
questions.explanation: Kerala is famous for its backwaters, coconut trees, and lush green scenery. It is called 'God's Own Country'.
questions.funFact: Kerala was the first state in India where 100% of people can read and write!

questions.question: What is the capital of West Bengal?
questions.explanation: Kolkata is the capital of West Bengal. It was the capital of India during the British period until 1911.
questions.funFact: Kolkata has the only operating tram network in all of India!

questions.question: Which state is the smallest in India?
questions.explanation: Goa is the smallest state in India by area. It is located on the western coast along the Arabian Sea.
questions.funFact: Even though Goa is the smallest state, it has some of the most famous beaches in the world!

questions.question: What is the capital of Punjab?
questions.explanation: Chandigarh is the capital of Punjab. Interestingly, it is also the capital of Haryana!
questions.funFact: Chandigarh is one of the best planned cities in India and was designed by a famous architect from France named Le Corbusier!

questions.question: Which state is famous for its tea gardens?
questions.explanation: Assam is very famous for its tea gardens. Assam tea is enjoyed by people all over the world.
questions.funFact: Assam produces more than half of all the tea grown in India!

questions.question: What is the capital of Rajasthan?
questions.explanation: Jaipur is the capital of Rajasthan. It is known as the 'Pink City' because many of its buildings are pink in colour.
questions.funFact: In 1876, the entire city of Jaipur was painted pink to welcome the Prince of Wales from England!

questions.question: Which state is shaped like a long thin strip and is famous for beaches?
questions.explanation: Goa is a small, narrow state on the western coast of India famous for its beautiful beaches and churches.
questions.funFact: Goa was ruled by the Portuguese for over 450 years before becoming part of India in 1961!

questions.question: What is the capital of Gujarat?
questions.explanation: Gandhinagar is the capital of Gujarat. It is named after Mahatma Gandhi, who was born in Gujarat.
questions.funFact: Many people think Ahmedabad is the capital because it is the biggest city, but the capital is actually Gandhinagar, which is right next to it!

questions.question: Which state is known for the lion?
questions.explanation: Gujarat is the only place in the world outside Africa where you can find Asiatic lions. They live in the Gir Forest.
questions.funFact: There are about 700 Asiatic lions living in Gujarat's Gir Forest, and their numbers are growing every year!

questions.question: What is the capital of Uttar Pradesh?
questions.explanation: Lucknow is the capital of Uttar Pradesh. It is known as the 'City of Nawabs' and is famous for its food and culture.
questions.funFact: Uttar Pradesh has the most people of any state in India — if it were a country, it would be the 5th most populated country in the world!

questions.question: Which state is famous for the dance form Bharatanatyam?
questions.explanation: Bharatanatyam is a classical dance form from Tamil Nadu. It is one of the oldest dance forms in India.
questions.funFact: Bharatanatyam is over 2,000 years old and was originally performed in temples!

questions.question: What is the capital of Bihar?
questions.explanation: Patna is the capital of Bihar. It is one of the oldest continuously inhabited cities in the world.
questions.funFact: The ancient name of Patna was Pataliputra, and it was the capital of the great Maurya Empire over 2,000 years ago!

questions.question: Which Indian state is famous for Kathakali dance?
questions.explanation: Kathakali is a famous classical dance form from Kerala. Dancers wear very colourful costumes and heavy makeup.
questions.funFact: Kathakali dancers spend hours putting on their makeup and costumes — sometimes it takes 3 to 4 hours just to get ready!

questions.question: What is the capital of Madhya Pradesh?
questions.explanation: Bhopal is the capital of Madhya Pradesh. It is known as the 'City of Lakes' because it has many beautiful lakes.
questions.funFact: Madhya Pradesh is called the 'Heart of India' because it is located right in the centre of the country!

questions.question: Which state is famous for the Bihu dance and festival?
questions.explanation: Bihu is a very popular festival and dance in Assam. It celebrates the Assamese New Year and the harvest season.
questions.funFact: During Bihu, people in Assam make special sweets called pitha from rice and coconut!

questions.question: What is the capital of Telangana?
questions.explanation: Hyderabad is the capital of Telangana. After Telangana was formed in 2014, Hyderabad served as the joint capital of both Telangana and Andhra Pradesh for 10 years.
questions.funFact: Hyderabad is called the 'City of Pearls' because it was once the global centre of the pearl trade!

questions.question: What is the capital of Andhra Pradesh?
questions.explanation: Amaravati is the designated capital of Andhra Pradesh. The state got a new capital after Hyderabad went to Telangana.
questions.funFact: The name Amaravati means 'place of immortals' and the area has a history going back to ancient Buddhist times!

questions.question: Which state has the one-horned rhinoceros as its state animal?
questions.explanation: The one-horned rhinoceros is the state animal of Assam. Kaziranga National Park in Assam is home to two-thirds of the world's one-horned rhinos.
questions.funFact: The Indian one-horned rhino can weigh as much as a car — about 2,000 kilograms!

questions.question: Which river flows through the state of Odisha and is known as the 'Sorrow of Odisha'?
questions.explanation: The Mahanadi river flows through Odisha and was called the 'Sorrow of Odisha' because it used to cause terrible floods.
questions.funFact: The Hirakud Dam on the Mahanadi is one of the longest dams in the world, stretching about 26 kilometres!

questions.question: What is the state animal of Madhya Pradesh?
questions.explanation: The Barasingha (swamp deer) is the state animal of Madhya Pradesh. It is found in Kanha National Park.
questions.funFact: Barasingha means 'twelve-horned' in Hindi because the male deer can have antlers with 12 or more points!

questions.question: What is the capital of Odisha?
questions.explanation: Bhubaneswar is the capital of Odisha. It is known as the 'Temple City' because it has hundreds of ancient temples.
questions.funFact: Bhubaneswar once had over 700 temples, earning it the nickname 'Ekamra Kshetra' or the City of Temples!

questions.question: Which state celebrates the famous Hornbill Festival?
questions.explanation: The Hornbill Festival is celebrated in Nagaland every December. It showcases the culture and traditions of Naga tribes.
questions.funFact: The festival is named after the hornbill bird, which is one of the most admired birds in Nagaland and appears in many tribal folk stories!

questions.question: Which state is the largest producer of spices in India?
questions.explanation: Kerala is the largest producer of spices in India. Pepper, cardamom, cinnamon, and cloves all grow abundantly there.
questions.funFact: Centuries ago, European explorers came all the way to Kerala looking for pepper, which was so valuable it was called 'Black Gold'!

questions.question: What is the capital of Sikkim?
questions.explanation: Gangtok is the capital of Sikkim. It is a beautiful hill town surrounded by mountains and monasteries.
questions.funFact: Sikkim became the first fully organic state in India in 2016, meaning all farming there is done without chemical fertilizers!

questions.question: Which famous river flows through Varanasi in Uttar Pradesh?
questions.explanation: The Ganga (Ganges) river flows through Varanasi. Varanasi is one of the oldest living cities in the world.
questions.funFact: The Ganga river travels about 2,525 kilometres from the Himalayas to the Bay of Bengal, passing through five states!

questions.question: Which state is known as the 'Spice Garden of India'?
questions.explanation: Kerala is known as the 'Spice Garden of India' because it grows a wide variety of spices including pepper, cardamom, and cloves.
questions.funFact: The spice trade from Kerala is what attracted the Portuguese explorer Vasco da Gama to India in 1498!

questions.question: What is the capital of Jharkhand?
questions.explanation: Ranchi is the capital of Jharkhand. Jharkhand was carved out of Bihar and became a separate state in the year 2000.
questions.funFact: Ranchi is known as the 'City of Waterfalls' because it has many beautiful waterfalls nearby, including the famous Hundru Falls!

questions.question: Which state celebrates Onam, the harvest festival?
questions.explanation: Onam is the biggest festival in Kerala. It celebrates the harvest season and the legendary King Mahabali.
questions.funFact: During Onam, families prepare a grand feast called Onam Sadhya that can have up to 26 different dishes, all served on a banana leaf!

questions.question: What is the capital of Himachal Pradesh?
questions.explanation: Shimla is the capital of Himachal Pradesh. It was the summer capital of British India because of its cool weather.
questions.funFact: Shimla has a toy train called the Kalka-Shimla Railway that passes through 102 tunnels and is a UNESCO World Heritage Site!

questions.question: Which state is the largest in India by area?
questions.explanation: Rajasthan is the largest state in India by area, covering about 342,239 square kilometres.
questions.funFact: The Thar Desert in Rajasthan is so big that it covers about 60% of the state and is home to many unique animals like the Great Indian Bustard!

questions.question: What is the state bird of Rajasthan?
questions.explanation: The Great Indian Bustard is the state bird of Rajasthan. It is one of the heaviest flying birds in the world.
questions.funFact: The Great Indian Bustard is critically endangered, with fewer than 150 left in the wild, and most of them are found in Rajasthan!

questions.question: Which state is famous for the Rann of Kutch, a vast white salt desert?
questions.explanation: The Rann of Kutch is in Gujarat. During full moon nights, the white salt desert looks like a magical silver landscape.
questions.funFact: The Rann of Kutch is so flat and white that it can be seen from space, and during monsoon it turns into a shallow wetland!

questions.question: What is the capital of Arunachal Pradesh?
questions.explanation: Itanagar is the capital of Arunachal Pradesh, a state in the far northeast of India. Arunachal Pradesh means 'Land of the Dawn-Lit Mountains'.
questions.funFact: Arunachal Pradesh is the first place in India to see the sunrise every day because it is the easternmost state!

questions.question: In which year was Telangana formed as a separate state from Andhra Pradesh?
questions.explanation: Telangana was formed on June 2, 2014, becoming the 29th state of India. It was carved out of the northwestern part of Andhra Pradesh.
questions.funFact: The Telangana movement for a separate state was one of the longest statehood movements in Indian history, lasting over 50 years!

questions.question: Which state has the highest population density in India?
questions.explanation: Bihar has the highest population density among Indian states, with over 1,100 people per square kilometre according to the 2011 Census.
questions.funFact: Despite being one of the most densely populated states, Bihar is also home to the ancient university of Nalanda, which was one of the first universities in the entire world!

questions.question: Which Indian state has the most number of official languages?
questions.explanation: Jammu and Kashmir recognises multiple official languages including Urdu, Kashmiri, Dogri, Hindi, and English, reflecting its diverse cultural heritage.
questions.funFact: India as a country recognises 22 official languages in its Constitution, making it one of the most linguistically diverse nations on Earth!

questions.question: Which northeastern state is the only state in India with a majority Christian population?
questions.explanation: Nagaland has the highest percentage of Christians among all Indian states, with about 88% of its population following Christianity.
questions.funFact: Nagaland is home to 16 major tribes, each with their own distinct language, dress, and traditions!

questions.question: Which river forms a natural boundary between Karnataka and Andhra Pradesh?
questions.explanation: The Tungabhadra River forms a natural boundary between parts of Karnataka and Andhra Pradesh. It is a tributary of the Krishna River.
questions.funFact: The ruins of Hampi, a UNESCO World Heritage Site and the capital of the Vijayanagara Empire, sit on the banks of the Tungabhadra River!

questions.question: Which state has the longest coastline in India?
questions.explanation: Gujarat has the longest coastline among Indian states at about 1,600 kilometres, stretching along the Arabian Sea.
questions.funFact: Gujarat's Alang ship-breaking yard is the largest in the world, where huge old ships are taken apart and recycled!

questions.question: Tulu is a language spoken primarily in which Indian state?
questions.explanation: Tulu is spoken mainly in the Dakshina Kannada and Udupi districts of Karnataka, in a region called Tulu Nadu.
questions.funFact: Tulu has its own ancient script called Tigalari, but today most Tulu speakers write using the Kannada script!

questions.question: Which state was the last to be reorganised into a Union Territory in 2019?
questions.explanation: In August 2019, Jammu and Kashmir was reorganised from a state into two Union Territories: Jammu & Kashmir and Ladakh.
questions.funFact: Ladakh, the other Union Territory created at the same time, contains the highest motorable road in the world at Umlingla Pass at 19,024 feet!

questions.question: The Chilika Lake, the largest coastal lagoon in India, is in which state?
questions.explanation: Chilika Lake is located in Odisha and is the largest coastal lagoon in India. It is a Ramsar wetland site of international importance.
questions.funFact: Every winter, over a million birds from as far as Siberia and Central Asia migrate to Chilika Lake, including beautiful pink flamingos!

questions.question: Which state has the highest literacy rate in India?
questions.explanation: Kerala has the highest literacy rate in India at about 96%. It was the first state to achieve 100% primary education.
questions.funFact: Kerala's focus on education goes back to the 19th century when the rulers of Travancore declared that the state should spend its resources on educating its people!

questions.question: The Bodo language is primarily spoken in which Indian state?
questions.explanation: Bodo is primarily spoken in Assam, mainly in the Bodoland Territorial Region. It is one of the 22 scheduled languages of India.
questions.funFact: The Bodo people have a vibrant spring festival called Bwisagu, which is celebrated with traditional dance, music, and feasting for a whole week!

questions.question: Which three states were created in November 2000 from existing states?
questions.explanation: Jharkhand (from Bihar), Chhattisgarh (from Madhya Pradesh), and Uttarakhand (from Uttar Pradesh) were all created in November 2000.
questions.funFact: Chhattisgarh is called the 'Rice Bowl of India' because it produces a huge amount of rice, while Jharkhand sits on some of the richest mineral deposits in the country!
